mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the industrial production index signals expanding corporate output and revenue growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive after monthly INDPRO increases (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 1.0 percent, further anticipated tightening by the Federal Reserve raises discount rates on future corporate cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds effective rate at 1.16% as of Nov 2017
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales indicate a contraction in both labor income and consumer spending, jointly depressing aggregate demand and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are nonnegative in more than 50% of months when both PAYEMS and RSAFS decline simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of low unemployment, month-over-month increases in housing starts signal overstretched construction activity and rising input cost pressures that precede equity market corrections, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment rate at 4.1% as of Nov 2017
regime_hint: tight labor market with unemployment below 4.5%
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns following HOUST increases are negative in fewer than 45% of observations when UNRATE is below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.25 percent signals inflationary pressure that causes the Federal Reserve to tighten policy faster than discounted by markets, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns following high CPI prints are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 4.5 percent signals a binding labor supply constraint that raises wage costs and squeezes corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when UNRATE is below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in retail sales and housing starts reflect synchronized consumer and residential investment strength that boosts aggregate demand and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are not significantly positive when both RSAFS and HOUST increase month-over-month (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output and lower corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following INDPRO declines are negative in fewer than 45% of instances.
